learnings.subject: Emergency Dematter Cream
learnings.learning: Dematter paid ROAS fell to RED (1.43 vs 2.07 breakeven) in the same window its 'detangler for dogs' organic rank slid 8->13 - possible pattern: rising paid spend masking organic decline; check organic rank before scaling Dematter paid.
evidence: ROAS 1.43 on $318.18 spend (Supermetrics Query B, 2026-07-08)
Organic rank 'detangler for dogs' 8->13 (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, 2026-07-08)

2026-07-10: 'detangler for dogs' rank slid further to 7 (from a baseline of 5), and 'dog detangler' dropped 9 spots (7->16) - organic instability continues, but paid ROAS remains unverifiable (Supermetrics down 2nd straight day) so this cannot be confirmed or contradicted today

2026-07-11: 'detangler for dogs' rank collapsed 7->23 (-16 spots), the largest single-day drop yet in this pattern (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, report date 2026-07-10); other Dematter keywords ('dog detangler', 'dematting comb for dogs', 'dematting spray for dogs') all improved the same day, so this is isolated to one keyword, not a whole-listing decline. Paid ROAS impact still unverifiable - Supermetrics 3rd consecutive day of total outage

2026-07-12: 'detangler for dogs' reversed sharply, 23->8 (+15 spots), the largest single-day recovery yet (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, report date 2026-07-11) - the keyword is now more volatile in both directions than any other Dematter keyword. Paid ROAS impact still unverifiable - Supermetrics 4th consecutive day of total outage. This does not confirm the original 'decline' direction, but does reinforce that this specific keyword is unusually unstable; driver remains unidentified without paid-spend visibility.

2026-07-13: 'detangler for dogs' and 'dog detangler' are absent entirely from today's Daily Keyword Tracker report (only 'dematting tool for dogs' and 'dematting comb for dogs' listed for this SKU) - report display gap, not a confirmed rank change either direction. Paid ROAS impact still unverifiable - Supermetrics 5th consecutive day of total outage.

2026-07-14: both keywords reappeared in the report (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, report date 2026-07-13) - 'detangler for dogs' rank 12 (-4) and 'dog detangler' rank 13 (-4), both down vs H10's own day-over-day indicator. No Step 0 baseline available since both were absent the prior day. Paid ROAS is now verifiable again (Supermetrics pipeline resolved) - Dematter ROAS 1.83 (YELLOW, not RED) on $300.75 spend/$551.76 attributed sales (Supermetrics Query B, date 2026-07-12), so today's organic dip is NOT coinciding with a paid ROAS collapse - continues to weaken the original 'masking' hypothesis while confirming the keyword's volatility.

2026-07-16 (data Jul 14-15): H10 Keyword Tracker (report date 2026-07-15) shows 'dog detangler' at organic rank 17 (-5 spots, SV 2,052), 'detangler for dogs' rank 14 (+4, SV 1,561), 'dematting spray for dogs' rank 3 (-1, SV 1,829), 'dematting comb for dogs' rank 22 (+4, SV 5,818). Mixed signals: 'dog detangler' continues its downward drift (-5) while 'detangler for dogs' recovered (+4). Paid ROAS on Jul 14: 1.993 (YELLOW, Supermetrics Query B) — still not in collapse alongside organic drift. Pattern remains inconclusive.

2026-07-17 (data Jul 15-16): H10 Keyword Tracker (report date 2026-07-16) shows 'detangler for dogs' at rank 17 (-3 spots from 14, SV 1,561) — 3-spot drop threshold triggered. 'dematting comb for dogs' at rank 26 (-4 spots from 22, SV 5,818) — 4-spot drop threshold triggered. 'dematting spray for dogs' stable at rank 3. 'dog detangler' rank 19 (-2). Additionally, Dematter paid ROAS on Jul 15: 1.578 (RED band, band change from YELLOW on Jul 14 — Supermetrics Query B, spend $157.35, attributed sales $248.29). Two Dematter organic keywords in simultaneous decline alongside paid ROAS dropping into RED — strengthens the original hypothesis vs. prior contradictions.

2026-07-18 (data Jul 16): Dematter ROAS 1.59 (RED band, $156.23 spend, $248.29 attributed sales — Supermetrics Query B, data date 2026-07-16). H10 Jul 17: 'detangler for dogs' -3 to rank 17 (SV 1,561), 'dematting comb for dogs' -4 to rank 26 (SV 5,818), 'dog detangler' -2 to rank 19 — three keywords declining simultaneously on same day as second consecutive RED paid ROAS. This is the second co-occurrence of organic decline + paid ROAS RED, strengthening the masking hypothesis further. Two data points of co-occurrence now (Jul 15 and Jul 16 data).

2026-07-19 (data Jul 17): Dematter ROAS 2.581 (YELLOW band, $274.27 spend, $708.09 attributed sales — Supermetrics Query B, first non-RED day after two consecutive RED days). H10 Jul 18: 'detangler for dogs' improved to rank 14 (+3 vs prior rank 17), 'dematting comb for dogs' improved to rank 23 (+3 vs prior rank 26) — BOTH keywords recovering simultaneously on the same day ROAS recovered to YELLOW. This is the reverse of the prior masking co-occurrence: improving ROAS coincides with improving organic ranks, further supporting a bidirectional correlation. The masking hypothesis (declining paid performance → organic decline) now has two co-occurrence data points in each direction (decline: Jul 15-16 data; recovery: Jul 17 data). TACOS remains extreme at 140.4% despite ROAS improvement.

2026-07-23 (data Jul 22): H10 Keyword Tracker — 'detangler for dogs' r14→r18 (-4 spots), 'dematting comb' r17→r19 (-2 spots). Both keywords declining again. Latest Supermetrics Query A account ROAS 2.3836 (Jul 21 data). Organic decline resumes alongside account-level ROAS in YELLOW.
learnings.action_implication: The bidirectional correlation (declining ROAS ↔ declining ranks; improving ROAS ↔ improving ranks) across 2+2 data points strengthens the hypothesis. However TACOS is 140.4% — paid spend is far above sustainable threshold despite YELLOW ROAS. Before scaling Dematter budget, verify TACOS trend normalizing. With paid ROAS now YELLOW (first recovery), do NOT immediately cut bids — observe 2 more days to confirm stability. Organic recovery (+3 on both tracked keywords) is a positive signal. If ROAS holds YELLOW for 2 consecutive days, consider revisiting Dematter as Meta hero SKU (per L-2026-07-16-002 recovery clause).

learnings.subject: Detangling Treatment / 4-in-1 Shampoo / Pin Brush / Rake Brush
learnings.learning: Low-spend SKUs ($3-$13/day) repeatedly spend with zero attributed sales - sub-$15 daily budgets may be too small to exit the learning phase and convert; trickle spend produces losses with nothing learned.
evidence: 2026-07-08: Detangling $11.51 / Shampoo $3.00 / Pin Brush $4.61, all ROAS 0.00 (Supermetrics Query B, 2026-07-08)

2026-07-14: same pattern confirmed after a 5-day Supermetrics outage, and now extends to a 4th SKU - Detangling Treatment $12.55, Pin Brush $5.05, Rake Brush $4.63, 4-in-1 Shampoo $2.60, all ROAS 0.00/KILL band (Supermetrics Query B, date 2026-07-12). These SKUs are well past the 3-day learning window (flagged since 7/8), so kill-band evaluation is action-eligible, not learning-suppressed.

2026-07-16 (data Jul 14): PARTIAL CONTRADICTION — Detangling Treatment ($11.40 spend) attributed ROAS 1.666 (RED, $18.99 sales) and Pin Brush ($9.45 spend) attributed ROAS 2.115 (YELLOW, $19.99 sales) — both converted on this date despite remaining in sub-$15/day budget range. Rake Brush ($4.06) and Shampoo ($1.10) remain KILL band (ROAS 0). The original 'always-zero' pattern does not hold universally — Detangling and Pin Brush can convert at these spend levels, even if inconsistently.

2026-07-17 (data Jul 15): Detangling Treatment ROAS 3.087 (GREEN band, $12.30 spend, $37.98 attributed sales) — band change RED→GREEN, consecutive conversion day. Pin Brush ROAS 0.00 (KILL band, $7.60 spend) — band change YELLOW→KILL, zero conversion after showing YELLOW the prior day. Rake Brush ($4.85, ROAS 0) and Shampoo ($2.10, ROAS 0) continue consistent KILL-band behavior. Detangling Treatment diverging toward reliable converter; Pin Brush showing high day-to-day ROAS variance; Rake and Shampoo consistent non-converters.

2026-07-18 (data Jul 16): Pin Brush $4.47 spend, $19.99 attributed — ROAS 4.47 (GREEN, above 2.61 breakeven). Shampoo $1.10 spend, $19.99 attributed — ROAS 18.17 (GREEN, above 3.75 breakeven). Detangling Treatment $7.80 spend, $0 — ROAS 0.00 (KILL band, flipped GREEN→KILL after GREEN on Jul 15). Rake Brush $3.99 spend, $0 — ROAS 0.00 (KILL). Pattern update: Pin Brush GREEN again (volatile but capable of high ROAS days), Shampoo GREEN (2nd conversion in recent data), Detangling Treatment reverted to KILL (inconsistent), Rake Brush consistent KILL.

2026-07-19 (data Jul 17): Pin Brush $9.14 spend, $39.98 attributed — ROAS 4.37 (GREEN, band change KILL→GREEN, 3rd GREEN day in last 5 data points). Shampoo $3.19 spend, $39.98 attributed — ROAS 12.53 (GREEN, band change KILL→GREEN). Rake Brush $5.15 spend, $14.99 attributed — ROAS 2.91 (YELLOW band, band change KILL→YELLOW, first non-KILL day). Detangling Treatment $17.49 spend, $0 — ROAS 0.00 (KILL band). Major band improvement day across the group on Jul 17 data.
learnings.action_implication: SKUs have diverged — treat individually, not as a group. Rake Brush: first YELLOW day (2.91 ROAS) — still below 3.12 breakeven but trending up; hold evaluation for 2 more data points before any budget change. Detangling Treatment: GREEN Jul 15, KILL Jul 16, KILL Jul 17 — 2 consecutive KILL days after one GREEN; do not scale until 3+ consecutive GREEN days. Pin Brush: KILL Jul 15, GREEN Jul 16, KILL→GREEN Jul 17 (ROAS 4.37) — volatile but 2 of 3 recent days GREEN; hold at current spend and monitor. Shampoo: GREEN Jul 16 (ROAS 18.17), GREEN Jul 17 (ROAS 12.53) — 2 consecutive GREEN days, above breakeven both times; monitor 1 more data point before budget increase.

learnings.subject: Account tracking hygiene
learnings.learning: ASINs spend in the ad account without existing in roas_bands.json - untracked spend can run unnoticed until the per-SKU pipeline works; every new campaign launch must add its ASIN to roas_bands.json the same day.
evidence: B0GT1H84JM $166.90 spend ROAS 1.41, unrecognized (Supermetrics Query B, 2026-07-08)

2026-07-14: confirmed after the 5-day Supermetrics outage and significantly escalated - B0GT1H84JM now spending $258.62/day (44% of the entire $586.72 account spend), still unrecognized. Additionally 7 MORE unrecognized ASINs appeared that were never seen before 2026-07-14.

2026-07-16 (data Jul 14): RESOLVED — roas_bands.json was updated (by Jul 14) to include all previously unrecognized ASINs including B0GT1H84JM (Save the Shave Bundle), B0GY9WZWKH, B0GXLPJS4C, and all bundle ASINs. Supermetrics Query B today returned 7 ASINs with spend, all recognized in roas_bands.json (zero unrecognized ASIN flags).
learnings.action_implication: Root cause addressed: roas_bands.json now covers all spending ASINs. Maintain the launch-checklist rule: campaign live → ASIN added to roas_bands.json same day. Remaining gap: B0FQFP2S9F (FurFix) is not in roas_bands.json — add it if/when it starts ad spend. Monitor each run for any new unrecognized ASINs as new products launch.

learnings.subject: Supermetrics schedule_id pipeline
learnings.learning: Root cause identified 2026-07-13 — TWO stacked failures: (1) Supermetrics schedule_ids expire before morning collection regardless of gap length; (2) the Amazon Ads data source (ds AA) hangs indefinitely on all report jobs. Fix (1) shipped: Evening Pre-Run v5 polls queries to completion at 11 PM and stores RESULTS in data/supermetrics_data.json; Morning Routine v10.6 reads the file, never redeems IDs. Fix (2) pending: Amazon Ads connection re-auth by Will at hub.supermetrics.com/token-management.
evidence: supermetrics_ids.json submitted_at: 2026-07-08 23:04:59 PT
All 6 schedule_ids (A-F) returned RuntimeError 'schedule_id has expired' on first poll, 2026-07-09 morning run
2026-07-10 through 2026-07-13: 5 consecutive days of total (6-for-6) outage across varying gap lengths (4h and 8h45m both failed)
2026-07-13 (v5 first run): Evening Pre-Run v5 polled to completion — D/E/F completed; A/B/C (ds AA) hung 43 min and never completed.
2026-07-14: Queries A/B/C (ds AA) all returned status 'completed' with real per-SKU data — first success after 5-day outage. One-day success, not yet conclusive.
2026-07-15: Queries A/B/C (ds AA) FAILED again — 'Query stalled: data source reported running but no tasks scheduled'. Regression from 7/14 single-day success.

2026-07-16 (morning run): supermetrics_data.json generated by Evening Pre-Run at 2026-07-15 23:07 PT was available and FRESH — all 6 queries (A-F) status 'completed' including A/B/C (ds AA). Store-results-not-IDs fix (1) is confirmed working.
learnings.action_implication: ARCHITECTURAL RULE (survives this incident): never build a submit-now/collect-later handoff on Supermetrics schedule_ids — poll to completion in the same session and persist DATA, not IDs. DIAGNOSTIC RULE: if ds AA jobs hang >40 min while ASELL/AW complete normally, suspect the Amazon Ads connection in Supermetrics (re-auth at hub.supermetrics.com/token-management). Supermetrics support trace IDs fa024804dae1583ed581fa313a30d9db and 56214a5ea32dfa604bda663bef5521d6 remain available if escalation is needed.

learnings.subject: Save the Shave UGC Bill (Meta campaign)
learnings.learning: Meta campaign 'Save the Shave UGC Bill _UGC_070306' breached the $75 cumulative-spend / ROAS<1.0 kill threshold on 2026-07-09 ($130.89 spend, ROAS 0.52) and was flagged for a kill/revise decision. As of 2026-07-10 it has continued spending past the threshold with no kill/revise decision recorded - the flag alone does not stop spend without a human action.
evidence: 2026-07-09: $130.89 7d spend, ROAS 0.52 (Meta MCP, flagged jarvis_metaads_2026-07-09)

2026-07-10 through 2026-07-19: 12 consecutive days with no human action recorded. L30D spend grew from $130.89 to $379.67. ROAS declined from 0.52 to 0.59 with intermittent brief improvements. CTR stable near 6% throughout (hook works; failure is post-click per L-2026-07-16-001).
2026-07-23: L30D spend $447.17 / ROAS 0.714 — Day 32+ no action. Account L30D total $504.04 / ROAS 0.634.
learnings.action_implication: VALIDATED (11 confirmations, 0 contradictions, 32+ days unresolved): A kill-threshold flag produces no spend reduction without a same-day human decision — the flag alone is not a control mechanism. This campaign has now spent ~$370+ above the kill threshold with ROAS in KILL band. This is not a watch situation — it requires an immediate @Will decision today. Recommend pause of the Meta Save the Shave campaign. Before killing: verify 'Save the Shave UGC Bill - Copy' (120249157571500118) has a budget assigned or the product goes dark on Meta. Also implement CAPI coverage (Meta opp score rec #2 — +3 pts, 89.9% lower CPR cited).

learnings.subject: Shopify D2C channel
learnings.learning: Shopify direct-to-consumer sales have been $0 for 4 consecutive days (Jul 7-10), coinciding with $0 email revenue on the only two active Klaviyo flows (both zero-revenue, low-volume). No paid or organic driver in this run's data explains the drought - it may simply reflect very low current Shopify traffic/marketing investment rather than a discrete failure.
evidence: Shopify daily_7d: Jul7 $0/0 orders, Jul8 $0/0, Jul9 $0/0, Jul10 $0/0 (ShopifyQL, ds=sales)
Klaviyo 7d: total_email_revenue_7d $0 across both active flows
2026-07-17 (data Jul 16): Klaviyo flow report (last 7 days) returned 0 recipients across ALL flows — all email activity had gone to zero. Browse Abandonment remains DRAFT.
2026-07-19 (data Jul 18): Shopify $0 / 0 orders. Klaviyo ALL flows 0 sends for 14+ days. D2C channel has no organic, email, or apparent Meta-driven revenue.

2026-07-23 (last 7d): Klaviyo 4 recipients total (Abandoned Cart 2, Welcome Series 1, Warranty 1) — flows are sending again at very low volume but 0 conversions and 0 flow revenue. Browse Abandonment still DRAFT. Shopify Jul 22: $74.96 / 4 orders / AOV $18.74.
learnings.action_implication: ESCALATION: Klaviyo flows are now sending again at very low volume (4 recipients last 7d) — slight improvement from zero-send, but 0 conversions and 0 revenue persist. Browse Abandonment activation (Wren fired) is the minimum action. Will or Angelo must audit Klaviyo list health, suppression lists, and the pixel/tracking integration. Every Shopify order is un-retargetable until this is resolved.

learnings.subject: Untracked Amazon SKU - FurFix De-Shed Brush
learnings.learning: A 7th Amazon product not in the routine's 6-SKU tracked list ('Skip The Groomer FurFix De-Shed Brush') appeared in the Weekly Amazon Profit report (Jul 6-12) marked 'New', with a net loss (-$23.95) and no revenue/units reported yet - it was launched without being added to the morning routine's tracked-SKU set.
evidence: Weekly Amazon Profit Report (Jul 6-12, 2026): FurFix (B0FQFP2S9F), Gross Revenue '-', Net Profit -$23.95 (tagged 'New'), Units '-'
2026-07-16: FurFix in H10 Inventory Insights with LIF ≤ 0 Days (stockout/no sales) and LTSF ≥ 181 Days — STRANDED in FBA per Jul 15 SC data.

2026-07-19 and 2026-07-23: FurFix again in H10 Inventory Insights with LIF ≤ 0 Days and LTSF ≥ 181 Days — status unchanged. SKU remains stranded/unsold with no ads running. Stranded inventory issue unresolved after 3+ weeks.
learnings.action_implication: Add B0FQFP2S9F to the Step 4 Helium 10 tracked-SKU list and to roas_bands.json if/when it starts spending on ads. Angelo/Will should confirm this is an intentional new-product launch. The STRANDED inventory status (LIF ≤ 0, LTSF ≥ 181d) has persisted for 3+ weeks — check SC for stranded inventory root cause (compliance issue, listing problem, or FBA receiving error). Action is overdue.

learnings.subject: Slicker Brush (liquidation SKU) receiving ad spend
learnings.learning: Slicker Brush is flagged in roas_bands.json as liquidate=true with an explicit note 'Any ad spend > $0 = immediate flag' (no ad funding). Despite this, it shows real ad spend today, the first day per-SKU Amazon Ads data has been available in 6 days - the underlying campaign/targeting was evidently never paused when the SKU was set to liquidate.
evidence: 2026-07-14: Slicker Brush (B0FQ628KCH) spend $1.05, ROAS 0.00 (Supermetrics Query B, date 2026-07-12)
2026-07-15 through 2026-07-19: spend confirmed $1.37/day in multiple Supermetrics Query B reads. 7d spend $7.37. Organic rank damage observed then partially reversed.

2026-07-21 (data Jul 21): Slicker spend $1.37 (Query B). 7d spend $5.48 (Query C). Violation persists — Day 32+. H10 Jul 22: 'slicker brush for dogs' r166→r177 (-11 spots). Organic rank damage accelerating.
learnings.action_implication: Violation confirmed active for 32+ consecutive days. $5.48 7d spend on a liquidation SKU with zero attributed sales. Organic rank now r177 (-11 spots Jul 22) — ad spend on a liquidating SKU is actively damaging its organic rank. @Will must immediately pause ALL campaigns and ad groups targeting B0FQ628KCH in Ad Console. This is policy enforcement, not a performance call. Until the campaign is confirmed paused for 3+ consecutive days (zero spend in Query B), this remains an active violation.

learnings.subject: QuickBooks MTD P&L sync lag
learnings.learning: QuickBooks' MTD revenue/COGS figures have been frozen at the same value for two consecutive daily runs despite Shopify and Amazon (H10) showing real sales activity on the intervening days - the QB connector appears to be lagging real transaction data by at least a day, possibly due to bank-feed or Amazon settlement import delay.
evidence: 2026-07-13 run: MTD period noted as 'Jul 1-12 (connector returned figures identical to yesterday's Jul 1-11 report - likely sync lag)', revenue $3,248.82

2026-07-14 run: MTD period Jul 1-13 still shows revenue $3,248.82 and COGS $1,441.47 - identical to the 2026-07-13 run's Jul 1-12 figures - despite Shopify and H10 reporting real revenue in that window. Operating expenses DID change day over day, so the lag is isolated to revenue/COGS.
2026-07-15 through 2026-07-19: 7th consecutive day of confirmed sync lag. QB MTD revenue $3,387.50 (Jul 1-19). H10 Jul 18: net $1,291 on 121 units — none posting to QB.
2026-07-23 run: QB MTD revenue $24,553.71 (Jul 1-22) — frozen since Jul 12 (sync lag Day 12). Cash $484,001.80 combined (checking split unconfirmed).
learnings.action_implication: Do not use QB MTD revenue as a same-day-accurate figure — cross-check against H10 Daily Profit for Amazon revenue and Shopify for D2C. The sync lag is now confirmed on Day 12 with no resolution. Use QB for cash balance (balance sheet) and expenses only; use H10 + Shopify for revenue. Verify checking vs MM split when cash approaches $25k alert threshold.

learnings.subject: FBA inventory retrieval — Chrome scrape dependency, ASELL no inventory fields
learnings.learning: FBA inventory data (stock levels by SKU) is retrieved via a direct Seller Central browser scrape (Step 5c) rather than through the Supermetrics ASELL data source. The Supermetrics ASELL data source returns sales and session metrics only and does not expose FBA inventory fields in its schema. When Chrome is unavailable (cloud execution environment), Step 5c degrades entirely and inventory_data.json is written with a degraded notice — no inventory data is collected that run.
evidence: 2026-07-15 (v10.8 first run): Chrome browser not available in cloud execution environment; Seller Central scrape (Step 5c) could not execute.

2026-07-16 through 2026-07-19: Chrome still unavailable across 4+ consecutive days. H10 Daily Profit email provides partial substitute: Inventory Insights section flags B0FQ4PY573 and B0FQFP2S9F with LIF ≤ 0 Days.
2026-07-23: Chrome still unavailable (DOCTRINE confirmed). H10 Inventory Insights shows FurFix and Detangling Spray still at LIF ≤ 0, LTSF ≥ 181.
learnings.action_implication: FBA inventory retrieval requires Chrome access to Seller Central — it cannot be satisfied by Supermetrics ASELL. In automated cloud runs without Chrome, inventory_data.json will be written as degraded every run (DOCTRINE — do not attempt workarounds). Options: (1) provision Chrome/browser in the cloud environment, (2) add a separate manual SC inventory check, (3) evaluate SP-API GetFBAInventory as a direct API alternative. PRIORITY items for manual check: B0FQ4PY573 (Detangling Spray, LIF ≤ 0) and B0FQFP2S9F (FurFix, LIF ≤ 0, STRANDED). Both have been flagged across 5+ consecutive runs with no resolution reported.

learnings.subject: Meta CTR vs. ROAS mismatch — Save the Shave UGC Bill
learnings.learning: Save the Shave UGC Bill has a 6.05% CTR (3× the 1%+ video benchmark) but L30D ROAS 0.64 — the creative hook is stopping the scroll but the post-click conversion chain (landing page, offer clarity, bundle price, checkout friction) is failing. This is a landing page/offer problem, not a creative hook problem.
evidence: 2026-07-16 Blaze 2F audit: CTR 6.05% (stable per prior watchdog data), Frequency 1.53 (below 3.0 fatigue threshold), Engagement Rate Ranking: Above Average. L30D ROAS 0.64 on $313.87 spend.
2026-07-18: L30D ROAS 0.63, CTR 5.97%. ROAS declined again (0.67→0.63), CTR remains near 6%. Post-click failure pattern confirmed × 2 data points.
2026-07-19: L30D ROAS 0.59, CTR stable near 6%. ROAS declined again (0.63→0.59), 6 consecutive periods of decline. Post-click failure pattern confirmed × 3 data points.
learnings.action_implication: Before launching new creative hooks, audit the Save the Shave Bundle landing page: URL accuracy, price clarity, mobile checkout experience, product-ad alignment. Consider testing Emergency Dematter (single product, lower price point, clearer rescue use case) vs. the bundle to isolate whether offer complexity or price is driving poor CVR. Do not conclude the hook is broken based on ROAS alone — CTR data confirms the hook works. Also implement CAPI event coverage.

learnings.subject: Emergency Dematter — cross-channel performance leader
learnings.learning: Emergency Dematter is the strongest performing STG SKU across both channels. Amazon: ROAS 1.993 (YELLOW, closest to breakeven of any actively-spending SKU), organic rank #3 for 'dematting spray for dogs' (SV 1,829). Meta: two RESCUE-format creative concepts already staged and production-ready.
evidence: 2026-07-16 Blaze 2F audit: Amazon Dematter ROAS 1.993 (Supermetrics Query B, Jul 14 data)
2026-07-17 update: Dematter ROAS fell to RED band (1.578) on Jul 15. Per action_implication threshold: if RED persists 2+ days, reconsider as hero SKU.
2026-07-18 CONTRADICTION (data Jul 16): Dematter ROAS 1.59 (RED band, 2nd consecutive RED day). 'Cross-channel performance leader' claim now CONTRADICTED on paid ROAS for 2+ consecutive days.

2026-07-19 RECOVERY (data Jul 17): Dematter ROAS 2.581 (YELLOW band). RED streak ended. H10 Jul 18: 'detangler for dogs' +3 to rank 14, 'dematting comb for dogs' +3 to rank 23. Single-day recovery insufficient to restart Meta hero designation — 2 consecutive YELLOW days needed.

2026-07-23 (data Jul 21): Dematter ROAS 2.116 (GREEN band, YELLOW→GREEN band change). 'detangler for dogs' r14→r18 (-4 organic drop). ROAS GREEN while organic keyword declining — interesting mixed signal for L-2026-07-08-001 bidirectional hypothesis.
learnings.action_implication: Dematter ROAS now GREEN (2.116) on Jul 21 data. This is 1 GREEN day. Per L-2026-07-08-001, 2 consecutive YELLOW/GREEN days are needed before revisiting Dematter as Meta hero SKU. Watch Jul 22 data (available tomorrow) for confirmation. TACOS at 138% means Amazon paid spend is far above target even at GREEN ROAS — any Meta Dematter launch would add cross-channel spend pressure.

learnings.subject: Blaze write path — Meta MCP
learnings.learning: Blaze write path VERIFIED — Meta MCP write path confirmed working. blaze_write_path_verified = true. Claude can create campaigns, ad sets, creatives, and ads via Meta MCP in the STG environment (account 2158768751536031). A-1 auto-kill and B-1 ad launches are unblocked.
evidence: 2026-07-19: blaze_write_path_verified = true confirmed in blaze.json. Blaze v1.4 ran successfully with CLASS_A_ACTIVE authority on 2026-07-19.
learnings.action_implication: Write path verified. Blaze can now auto-execute A-1 kills and B-1 ad launches without requiring 'approved' reply in Slack, provided CLASS_A_ACTIVE authority is confirmed.

learnings.subject: Meta audience age distribution — 65+ over-indexed at near-breakeven, 45-54 worst performer
learnings.learning: Advantage+ is allocating 66% of budget to the 65+ age segment at ROAS 0.96 (near-breakeven, but still below 1.0). The 45-54 segment is the worst performer at ROAS 0.31. The target 25-54 demographic is not receiving the majority of budget.
evidence: 2026-07-16 Blaze 2F audit: 65+ receiving 66% of budget at ROAS 0.96. 45-54 receiving budget at ROAS 0.31 (worst age segment). Account targeting spec: US 25-54, all genders. Advantage+ is ignoring the intent of the targeting spec.
NOTE: This learning was referenced in the Jul 17 Blaze pass close-out as written, but was absent from learnings.json — recovered and formally added Jul 18 Blaze pass.
learnings.action_implication: Consider adding an age exclusion (exclude 65+) on a future ad set test to redirect budget to converting age segments within 25-54. However, prioritize the gender split finding first — male vs. female is the clearest ROAS signal (2.34 vs. 0.50). An age exclusion test is lower priority than BLZ-20260717-001 (male-targeted).

learnings.subject: Emergency Dematter — single-day ROAS collapse to kill floor
learnings.learning: Dematter ROAS collapsed from 2.581 (Jul 17) to 1.133 (Jul 18) — a 56% single-day drop — placing it $0.007 below the kill floor of 1.14. Pattern: Dematter exhibits extreme single-day ROAS volatility; do not pause based on one KILL-band day; confirm trend over 2-3 days before bid changes.
evidence: ROAS 2.581 on $274.27 spend Jul 17 (Supermetrics Query B, retrieved 2026-07-19)
ROAS 1.133 on Jul 18 spend — below kill floor 1.14 (Supermetrics Query B, retrieved 2026-07-20)
Kill floor = max(2.07 * 0.55, 1.0) = 1.14; ROAS 1.133 is $0.007 under
TACOS still elevated at 115.9% (7d) vs 35% target
Hypothesis: single-day attribution delay or order cancellations compressed Jul 18 ROAS
learnings.action_implication: Do not act on single-day per-SKU ROAS for Emergency Dematter. The 14d same-SKU attribution window produces extreme single-day swings on high-spend SKUs where one day without an attributed sale reads as KILL. Evaluate Dematter ROAS over a 7-day rolling window minimum. Flag only when Query A account-level ROAS also drops below 1.5 for 3+ consecutive days.

learnings.subject: Pin Brush + Shampoo — GREEN-to-KILL in one day on low-volume SKUs
learnings.learning: Pin Brush (ROAS 4.37 Jul 17 → 0.000 Jul 18) and 4-in-1 Shampoo (ROAS 12.53 Jul 17 → 0.000 Jul 18) both showed GREEN then KILL in consecutive days. Pattern: low-volume SKUs (<5 units/day) regularly report zero attributed sales on individual days due to Amazon's 14-day attribution window lag.
evidence: Pin Brush ROAS 4.37 Jul 17, ROAS 0.000 Jul 18 (Supermetrics Query B)
Shampoo ROAS 12.53 Jul 17, ROAS 0.000 Jul 18 with $1.10 spend (Supermetrics Query B)
Jul 18 units from H10 daily: Pin 6 units, Shampoo 3 units — volume consistent with prior days
learnings.action_implication: For low-volume SKUs spending less than $10/day (Pin Brush, Shampoo), never classify as KILL based on single-day Query B. Apply a 14-day cumulative evaluation window. Flag only if: cumulative 14-day spend >= $75 AND cumulative 14-day attributed sales = $0.

learnings.subject: Dashboard blank panels — missing `available` field in connectors_data.json sections
learnings.learning: Three sections (helium10, helium10_profits, amazon_ads) were written to connectors_data.json by v10.15 without the `available: true/false` key as the first field in each object. The dashboard render() function checks `available` before rendering each section — a missing key causes a blank panel even when all other data is correctly populated.
evidence: 2026-07-23: v10.15 connectors_data.json had helium10, helium10_profits, and amazon_ads sections without the `available` key — dashboard panels for those three sections displayed as blank on Jul 23 morning.
v10.16 critical fix applied: all sections now include `available: true/false` as their FIRST key. Full run re-executed under user override.
learnings.action_implication: ARCHITECTURAL RULE: every section object in connectors_data.json MUST have `available: true/false` as its FIRST key. The dashboard render() function checks this field before rendering — omitting it is equivalent to writing no data for that section. Enforce this rule in all future v10.x releases as a pre-write validation step.
